title: inga sertulifera subsp. sertulifera
intro: Inga sertulifera subsp. sertulifera, also known as the Inga tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Inga sertulifera subsp. sertulifera include treating fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from pain and swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
